TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-19-2013 IN 504, DEFENDANT BARE ESCENTUALS BEAUTY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE REASONABLENESS OF DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT AND RETALIATION BASED ON EVENTS PRIOR TO HER TERMINATION. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE A VIABLE WRONGFUL TERMINATION CLAIM, THAT WOULD NOT DISPOSE OF ANY ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION. MR. BRYANT IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (504)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BARE ESCENTUALS BEAUTY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances: Shelley G. Bryant of Bryant Whitten, LLP (559.494.4910) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Mark T. Kobata of Barlow Kobata & Denis LLP (310.277.7556) appearing for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Plaintiff has sufficiently raised a triable issue of material fact as to the reasonableness of Defendant's conduct and retaliation based on events prior to her termination. Even assuming that Plaintiff does not have a viable wrongful termination claim, that would not dispose of any entire cause of action. Mr. Bryant is directed to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CCP 437 c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Per agreement of the parties and order of the presiding judge, trial date in this is continued to September 30, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 206. Judge: Marla J.
